name: Pizza with Rosemary and Garlic (Pizza Bianca)
recipeIngredient: 4 cups flour (approximately): unbleached, or a 50-50 blend
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine warm water and yeast. Allow to dissolve.
2. Add 1 cup flour and stir constantly in one direction for about one minute.
This helps develop the gluten and forms a sponge.
3. Cover the bowl and let stand for half an hour up to three hours.
4. Sprinkle salt and oil over the dough, then add more flour by the half cup,
stirring constantly until the dough is heavier and harder to stir.
5. Knead the dough on a floured surface until smooth and easy to work with
(about 10 minutes).
6. Place the dough in a lightly oiled bread bowl, cover with plastic wrap,
and let rise until more than doubled (about three hours).
7. If baking is delayed, gently press down on the dough and let it rise again.
For longer storage, refrigerate the dough in a plastic bag for up to five days.
8. When ready to bake, lightly flour your work surface and prepare a 12 by
18-inch baking sheet.
9. Preheat the oven to 450 degrees Fahrenheit.
10. Turn the dough onto the prepared baking sheet and gently stretch it into
a rectangular shape that fits the sheet.
11. Brush the pizza with oil and sprinkle with sea salt and optional rosemary leaves.
12. Place the baking sheet on the bottom rack of the preheated oven and bake
until the crust around the edge is lightly golden (about 5 to 8 minutes).
13. Remove from the oven, brush the edges lightly with olive oil if desired,
and cut into squares.
14. Serve warm or hot. For reheating, place in a dampened paper bag at 300 degrees
Fahrenheit for about 10 minutes.
Yield: one large 12 by 18-inch pizza or two medium, or 3 to 4 small pizzas
